questions.prompt: Why pin GitHub Actions by SHA instead of tag?

choices.text: Tags are mutable — the action maintainer can move `@v4` to point at any commit at any time, including a malicious one if their account is compromised. SHAs are immutable references to specific code. With `uses: actions/checkout@v4`, you re-fetch whatever's at v4 every workflow run; if v4 gets moved (or the maintainer is compromised), your next build runs different code than yesterday's, with full repo access and possibly secrets. With `uses: actions/checkout@b4ffde65...` (SHA), you always run the exact code you reviewed; even a compromised maintainer can't change what your workflow runs. The cost: harder to upgrade (need to update the SHA + recheck). Tools like Dependabot automate this — it watches actions and proposes SHA bumps via PR, which you review. Standard hardened-CI practice in 2026; pinning by tag is the GitHub Actions equivalent of `npm install` without a lockfile.

questions.explanation: Tags are mutable; SHA pinning is immutable. Compromised maintainer can't change what your CI runs.

questions.prompt: A critical CVE drops in lodash. How fast can your team answer 'are we affected, and where?'

choices.text: Should be seconds with proper SBOM tooling. SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) is an inventory of every dependency in your build — direct and transitive — generated automatically per release. Tools: Syft, CycloneDX CLI, GitHub's dependency graph. When a CVE drops, grep the SBOM: `lodash@4.17.20` → 'yes, three services, here's the version.' Without SBOM, when Log4Shell hit, teams spent days frantically searching repos to figure out exposure — many missed transitive uses entirely. The work to set up SBOM is small (one command in your release pipeline); the payoff during the next critical CVE is massive. Combined with Dependabot/Renovate for automated patch PRs, the response is 'merge the bot's PR; deploy; done.' This is what production-grade supply-chain hygiene looks like.

questions.explanation: Seconds with SBOM tooling (Syft, CycloneDX). Grep the bill of materials. Critical for fast CVE response.
